label: Deduplicate
description: Idempotency gate. Computes a key from the configured expression and drops (or errors on) items whose key has already been seen — within the current execution by default, or across executions with an optional TTL window.

settings.name: key
settings.label: Key expression
settings.description: Template that resolves to the de-duplication key for each item. Items with an identical resolved key are treated as duplicates. Must be non-empty for every item.

settings.name: scope
settings.label: Scope
settings.description: execution = dedup only within the current flow run (idempotent fan-out). global = dedup across all executions (use with a TTL to suppress repeated webhook deliveries).

settings.name: ttlSeconds
settings.label: TTL (seconds)
settings.description: How long a seen key is remembered. 0 = forever within the scope. With a TTL, a key re-appearing after the window is treated as new again.

settings.name: onDuplicate
settings.label: On duplicate
settings.description: drop = return { duplicate:true } so downstream can skip; error = throw a runtime error on the second sighting.

ai.shortDescription: Drop items whose key was already processed — an idempotency / de-dup gate.
ai.whenToUse: Place inside a loop / map_reduce body or downstream of a webhook trigger to ensure each unique record (or delivery) is processed at most once. Route the duplicate path to a skip / no-op.
ai.examplePrompt: deduplicate incoming records by input.id so each id is processed only once per run
ai.promptHints: Output: {{steps.X.duplicate}} (true = already seen, downstream should skip), {{steps.X.firstSeen}} (true = first sighting, proceed). Use onDuplicate=drop with a condition node on {{steps.X.duplicate}} to branch.

outputAssertions.name: dedup_envelope
outputAssertions.errorMessage: dedup output is missing the { duplicate, key } envelope.
